cases.passage: Rahul Corporation issued 10,000, 10% debentures of Rs. 100 each on January 1, 2020. The debentures were redeemable at a premium of 5% on January 1, 2022. The company provided for the debentures redemption fund by transferring Rs. 1,50,000 to debenture redemption reserve on December 31, 2021. The company also declared an interim dividend of Rs. 20,000 on December 31, 2021. The balance of debenture redemption fund as on December 31, 2021, was Rs. 5,00,000. How will the company treat the debenture redemption fund in its balance sheet as on December 31, 2021?

questions.q: How will the company treat the debenture redemption fund in its balance sheet as on December 31, 2021?
questions.explanation: Debenture redemption fund is a part of non-current liability, thus it will be shown under equity and funds.

questions.q: What is the amount to be transferred to debenture redemption reserve on December 31, 2021?
questions.explanation: As per the given information, the company provided for the debentures redemption fund by transferring Rs. 1,50,000 to debenture redemption reserve on December 31, 2021.

questions.q: What is the balance of debenture redemption fund as on December 31, 2021?
questions.explanation: As per the given information, the balance of debenture redemption fund as on December 31, 2021, was Rs. 5,00,000.

questions.q: What is the amount of debentures redemption at a premium of 5%?
questions.explanation: As per the given information, the debentures were redeemable at a premium of 5% on January 1, 2022. The amount of debentures redemption will be 10,00,000 + (10,00,000 * 5/100) = Rs. 10,50,000.

questions.q: What is the amount of interim dividend declared by the company on December 31, 2021?
questions.explanation: As per the given information, the company also declared an interim dividend of Rs. 20,000 on December 31, 2021.

cases.passage: ABC Ltd. issued 5,000, 12% debentures of Rs. 100 each on April 1, 2019. The debentures were redeemable at a premium of 10% on April 1, 2022. The company provided for the debentures redemption fund by transferring Rs. 1,20,000 to debenture redemption reserve on March 31, 2022. The balance of debenture redemption fund as on March 31, 2022, was Rs. 4,80,000. The company also declared an interim dividend of Rs. 12,000 on March 31, 2022. The company's profit for the year ended March 31, 2022, was Rs. 2,50,000.

questions.q: How will the company treat the debenture redemption fund in its balance sheet as on March 31, 2022?
questions.explanation: Debenture redemption fund is a part of non-current liability, thus it will be shown under equity and funds.

questions.q: What is the amount to be transferred to debenture redemption reserve on March 31, 2022?
questions.explanation: As per the given information, the company provided for the debentures redemption fund by transferring Rs. 1,20,000 to debenture redemption reserve on March 31, 2022.

questions.q: What is the balance of debenture redemption fund as on March 31, 2022?
questions.explanation: As per the given information, the balance of debenture redemption fund as on March 31, 2022, was Rs. 4,80,000.

questions.q: What is the amount of debentures redemption at a premium of 10%?
questions.explanation: As per the given information, the debentures were redeemable at a premium of 10% on April 1, 2022. The amount of debentures redemption will be 5,00,000 + (5,00,000 * 10/100) = Rs. 5,50,000.

questions.q: What is the amount of interim dividend declared by the company on March 31, 2022?
questions.explanation: As per the given information, the company also declared an interim dividend of Rs. 12,000 on March 31, 2022.
